steps.title: The Drawing Endgame
steps.text: Opposite-colored bishops (one on light, one on dark) create notoriously drawish endgames. Even 2 extra pawns often aren't enough to win — the weaker side can just block on his bishop's color.

steps.title: Exception: With Queens
steps.text: PARADOX: in the MIDDLEGAME, opposite-colored bishops FAVOR the attacker. If you're attacking, your bishop has no opposite-color defender. Many brilliant attacks exploit this imbalance.
